creator: Ram Iyer
title: (No AI) Meta’s new AI chips will begin production in September
dc:creator: Ram Iyer
content: The company is taking a modular approach to designing these chips, anticipating that their needs will change as AI evolves rapidly by the time the chips are in production.
contentSnippet: The company is taking a modular approach to designing these chips, anticipating that their needs will change as AI evolves rapidly by the time the chips are in production.

creator: Russell Brandom
title: (No AI) Nvidia is a victim of the compute marketplace it created
dc:creator: Russell Brandom
content: Having proven how valuable compute can be, the company finds itself at the center of a market everyone wants to be in — while simpler technologies and less interesting companies get rich on the sidelines.
contentSnippet: Having proven how valuable compute can be, the company finds itself at the center of a market everyone wants to be in — while simpler technologies and less interesting companies get rich on the sidelines.

title: (No AI) Microsoft’s patch Tuesdays are about to get bigger
author: Stevie Bonifield
content:
![Illustration of Microsoft’s Windows logo](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/chorus/uploads/chorus_asset/file/24038601/acastro_STK109_microsoft_02.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Windows 11 updates could soon include fixes for more security issues at once. Microsoft said in [a blog post](https://blogs.windows.com/windowsexperience/?p=180398) on Thursday that it's now using AI to "identify potential issues earlier," which means "customers will see a higher volume of security updates included in each security release."

Hackers, even amateurs, have [increasingly been using AI](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/915660/mythos-script-kiddies-hackers-attack-cybersecurity-ai) to quickly exploit security weaknesses over the past several months. Security researchers are also using AI to find issues faster, leading to more frequent high-severity vulnerabilities, like the ["Copy Fail" exploit](https://www.theverge.com/tech/922243/linux-cve-2026-3141-copy-fail-exploit) that impacted nearly every Linux distribution in May. Similarly, when Anthropic announce …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/963307/microsoft-patch-tuesday-ai-security-updates)

Hackers, even amateurs, have increasingly been using AI to quickly exploit security weaknesses over the past several months. Security researchers are also using AI to find issues faster, leading to more frequent high-severity vulnerabilities, like the "Copy Fail" exploit that impacted nearly every Linux distribution in May. title: (No AI) OpenAI rolls out GPT-5.6 after government greenlight — and announces ‘ChatGPT Work’
author: Hayden Field
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/02/STK155_OPEN_AI_4_CVirginia_A.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

About two weeks after OpenAI's GPT-5.6 was caught up in [regulatory drama](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/957845/openai-gpt-5-6-trump-administration-ai-preview) - rolled out only to government-approved organizations during a "limited preview" period - the company has received the Trump administration's greenlight for a public rollout of the model. OpenAI CEO Sam Altman [called it](https://x.com/sama/status/2075266471316615436?s=20) "the best model we have ever produced."

To celebrate, OpenAI also unveiled a new AI agent on the same day: ChatGPT Work. It's billed as a combination of ChatGPT and Codex, allowing the everyday non-technical user to take advantage of Codex's capabilities for non-coding tasks, and it's powered by the GPT-5.6 model suite (Sol, Terra, and Luna). "It can g …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/963464/openai-gpt-5-6-codex-chatgpt-work)

To celebrate, OpenAI also unveiled a new AI agent on the same day: ChatGPT Work. title: (No AI) GPT-5.6: Frontier intelligence that scales with your ambition
content: More intelligence from every token, stronger performance per dollar, and more capability on demand for your hardest work.
contentSnippet: More intelligence from every token, stronger performance per dollar, and more capability on demand for your hardest work.

title: (No AI) ChatGPT is now a partner for your most ambitious work
content: ChatGPT Work is an agent that can take action across your apps and files, stay with a project for hours if needed, and turn a goal into finished work.
contentSnippet: ChatGPT Work is an agent that can take action across your apps and files, stay with a project for hours if needed, and turn a goal into finished work.

creator: Chris Stokel-Walker
title: (No AI) The $28 Million Mistake That Inspired Estonia’s AI ‘Fuckup Finder’
dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker
content: A single wording mistake cost the government millions. Now Estonia is using AI to spot legal errors before they become law—and to automate more of the state.
contentSnippet: A single wording mistake cost the government millions. Now Estonia is using AI to spot legal errors before they become law—and to automate more of the state.
